response.justification: Szabo demonstrates exceptional technical depth with 20+ years of software development experience and strong expertise in developer tools, testing infrastructure, and code quality—all relevant to the role's domain. However, the position explicitly requires 2+ years of people management experience, and the resume contains no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, 1-on-1s, or leading teams. His current Staff Engineer role appears focused on individual technical contribution rather than people leadership. The absence of this critical requirement makes him unsuitable for a full-time people-leadership management position, regardless of technical credentials.
strengths: 20+ years of progressive technical experience with multiple senior/staff roles and deep expertise in developer tools, CI/CD infrastructure, testing automation, and code quality

Proven passion for developer productivity through building open-source tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts), extensive testing framework knowledge, and GitLab/Docker/Artifactory infrastructure experience
Strong mentorship and knowledge-sharing track record demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, organizing Refactoring Dojos, and building educational content around software craftsmanship
concerns: No demonstrated people management experience—no mention of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or leading organizational initiatives required for manager role
Current Staff Engineer position appears focused on individual technical contribution (full-stack engineering, AI-agent infrastructure) rather than team leadership or cross-team coordination
Limited evidence of strategic roadmapping, setting team priorities, or driving organizational change—skills central to mid-to-senior management responsibilities
